hero.headline: Salt Lake’s pizza joint.
hero.body: Hand-rolled dough. Real mozzarella. A booth, a slice, a frosted Hires next door. That’s the deal. That’s always been the deal.
story.headline: Walk in.
story.p1: There’s a building on East 400 South that’s been making the same pizza for sixty years. Sticky booths in the best way. The cheese pulls. The cut is still done by hand. On Friday nights the line goes out the door and nobody complains, because the food is worth it.
story.p2: Litzas isn’t a date-night place. It’s not a reservation place. It’s the place your dad took you. It’s where the team eats after the game. It’s the one your kids will bring their kids to.
story.p3: You don’t need a menu to know what you want. But there is one if you want it.
menu.headline: Twenty-three pizzas.
menu.headline_slab: Hand-rolled in the morning. Baked when you order.
menu.body: A few favorites below. Tap a size to see prices. The full menu is one click away.
bridge.headline: Same parking lot.
bridge.p1: In Salt Lake, Litzas shares a parking lot with Hires Big H. In Midvale, we share a building. Pour yourself a root beer in a frosted mug. Eat your pizza. It’s been the deal since 1965.
loc.headline: Two shops.
loc.body: Walk in. Call ahead. We’ll have a pie waiting.
